title: The Name of the Rose
author: Umberto Eco
excerpt: A medieval monk investigates a series of murders in a wealthy Italian abbey, uncovering a labyrinth of theological intrigue, forbidden knowledge, and the power of laughter.
keyIdeas: The pursuit of truth is always entangled with power, ideology, and institutional authority
Laughter and comedy are subversive forces that threaten rigid systems of belief
Knowledge can be dangerous—but the suppression of knowledge is more dangerous still
The meaning of a text is never fixed; interpretation is an endless process
keyTakeaways: Great books reward multiple readings—new meanings emerge each time
Intellectual freedom requires the courage to question authority
The funniest ideas are often the most dangerous to established power
History is always more complex than the stories we tell about it
whoShouldRead: Lovers of literary detective fiction with intellectual depth
Readers interested in medieval history, theology, and philosophy
Fans of Jorge Luis Borges, whose influence pervades the novel
Anyone who enjoys books about books and the power of libraries
whoShouldSkip: Those put off by extensive Latin quotations and theological debates
Readers wanting clear-cut resolutions to every mystery
